title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During their train journey, Bobby Jones and Frankie (Frances) DeWinter discuss Bobby's intention to invest in a business called Bajee, which is run by a man named Baz. Bobby reveals that he left the navy due to eye issues caused by intense light during his overseas service, though the condition was not severe enough to prevent his continued service. Later, Frankie agrees to pick up Marquis Bolt from Selhurst at 5:00 pm. During their conversation, Frankie casually suggests the possibility that someone may have pushed a man down the cliff, questioning if it was murder rather than an accident.

The following day, an inquest is held regarding the death of Alexander Prichard. Dr. Thomas testifies that although the deceased had a pulse when found, he died from a broken spine after falling approximately fifty to sixty feet onto rocks. The doctor suggests the death was likely an accident caused by fog obscuring the cliff edge, though he notes suicide remains a possibility. Robert Jones testifies that while playing golf, he heard a loud shout that sounded like someone being hit by a ball or stepping into thin air, though he found his golf ball about a hundred yards away from the path.

Amelia Kemm, Alexander’s sister, testifies that he had recently returned from the East and was planning a hiking trip in Wales. She describes him as cheerful and energetic, noting he did not seem to have financial difficulties. When questioned about his lack of luggage, she explains that he disliked backpacks and intended to send parcels; she mentions a parcel containing his overnight essentials was misaddressed to Derbyshire instead of Leicestershire.
